question.label: Wanted opportunities
question.name: What opportunities have you wanted but not said out loud?
question.description: Turns his admission into a concrete aspiration so the conversation names the roles, scope, or exposure he actually wants.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.

assessment.note: He names a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which signals ambition and some positive energy.

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Delegation boundary
new_queue.name: What work are you still holding that should probably sit with others?

new_queue.label: First growth move
new_queue.name: Given what we’ve covered, what do you want to change first?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
